root_text.an3.52:1.1: Atha kho dve brāhmaṇā jiṇṇā vuddhā mahallakā addhagatā vayoanuppattā vīsavassasatikā jātiyā yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdiṁsu. Ekamantaṁ nisinnā kho te brāhmaṇā bhagavantaṁ etadavocuṁ:
root_text.an3.52:1.2: “mayamassu, bho gotama, brāhmaṇā jiṇṇā vuddhā mahallakā addhagatā vayoanuppattā vīsavassasatikā jātiyā;
root_text.an3.52:1.4: Ovadatu no bhavaṁ gotamo, anusāsatu no bhavaṁ gotamo yaṁ amhākaṁ assa dīgharattaṁ hitāya sukhāyā”ti.
root_text.an3.52:2.1: “Taggha tumhe, brāhmaṇā, jiṇṇā vuddhā mahallakā addhagatā vayoanuppattā vīsavassasatikā jātiyā;
root_text.an3.52:2.3: Āditto kho ayaṁ, brāhmaṇā, loko jarāya byādhinā maraṇena.
root_text.an3.52:2.4: Evaṁ āditte kho, brāhmaṇā, loke jarāya byādhinā maraṇena, yo idha kāyena saṁyamo vācāya saṁyamo manasā saṁyamo, taṁ tassa petassa tāṇañca leṇañca dīpañca saraṇañca parāyaṇañcāti.

translation_text.an3.52:1.1: Then two old brahmins—elderly and senior, who were advanced in years and had reached the final stage of life, being a hundred and twenty years old—went up to the Buddha, bowed, sat down to one side, and said to the Buddha:
translation_text.an3.52:1.2: “We brahmins, worthy Gotama, are old, elderly and senior, we’re advanced in years and have reached the final stage of life; we’re a hundred and twenty years old.
translation_text.an3.52:1.3: And we haven’t done what is good and skillful, nor have we made a shelter from fear.
translation_text.an3.52:1.4: Advise us, worthy Gotama, instruct us! It will be for our lasting welfare and happiness.”
translation_text.an3.52:2.1: “Indeed, brahmins, you’re old, elderly and senior.
translation_text.an3.52:2.2: And you haven’t done what is good and skillful, nor have you made a shelter from fear.
translation_text.an3.52:2.3: This world is burning with old age, sickness, and death.
translation_text.an3.52:2.4: But restraint here by way of body, speech, and mind is the shelter, asylum, island, refuge, and final haven for the departed.
translation_text.an3.52:3.1: When your house is on fire,
translation_text.an3.52:4.1: And as the world is on fire
translation_text.an3.52:5.4: as the good deeds done while living.”

variant_text.an3.52:4.1: Evaṁ āditto kho → evaṁ ādīpito (bj, pts1ed); evaṁ ādittake (sya-all, km)
